title: app/page.tsx - DocMed Landing Page
content:
This file defines the main landing page for the DocMed application.

## Purpose

The `app/page.tsx` file is responsible for rendering the user-facing landing page of the DocMed website. It brings together various components to showcase the product's features, explain its functionality, and guide users towards signing up.

## Key Components and Exports

This file exports a single `Home` component, which is the main entry point for the page. It orchestrates the rendering of the following components:

-   **`Navbar`**: A sticky navigation bar with a logo, navigation links, and a "Get Started" button. It also includes a mobile-responsive menu.
-   **`Hero`**: The prominent hero section featuring a dynamic globe animation, a compelling headline, and call-to-action buttons.
-   **`TrustBar`**: A section displaying key technologies and trust signals like "GitHub App" and "AI-Powered PRs".
-   **`Features`**: A detailed section outlining the core features of DocMed, each with an icon, title, description, and a highlight.
-   **`HowItWorks`**: A step-by-step explanation of how to get started with DocMed, emphasizing its simplicity and automation.
-   **`Stats`**: A visually engaging section presenting key statistics about the effectiveness and efficiency of DocMed.
-   **`CTA`**: A strong call-to-action section designed to capture user emails for early access or sign-up.
-   **`Footer`**: The standard website footer containing the logo and copyright information.

## Animation and UI Elements

-   Uses `framer-motion` for smooth animations, including fade-up effects, staggering animations for lists, and hover/tap effects on interactive elements.
-   Leverages Tailwind CSS for styling and layout.
-   Integrates UI components from `@/components/ui/globe-hero` for the hero section.
-   Utilizes `lucide-react` for various icons.

## Usage Example

This file is the root page component for the `/` route in a Next.js application. When a user navigates to the application's root URL, this `Home` component will be rendered, displaying the entire landing page structure.

```tsx
// app/page.tsx

// ... imports and component definitions ...

export default function Home() {
  return (
    <main>
      <Navbar />
      <Hero />
      <TrustBar />
      <Features />
      <HowItWorks />
      <Stats />
      <CTA />
      <Footer />
    </main>
  );
}
```
